steps.title: Place though in clause one
steps.content: The first independent clause is "Nakalembe doesn't just compile the information, though." The second is "she also shares her findings with African farmers." A semicolon must separate the two clauses.

steps.title: Reject bad clause joins
steps.content: Choice B joins independent clauses with only a comma. Choice C makes though start a dependent clause after a semicolon. Choice D leaves the two clauses without the needed boundary. The answer is A.
